Package org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.rewriters

Class AbstractAutoRewrites

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.reasonerInputs.EmptyInputReasoner
org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.rewriters.AbstractAutoRewrites
All Implemented Interfaces:
IReasoner
Direct Known Subclasses:
AutoRewrites, HideOriginalRewrites, ShowOriginalRewrites, TypeRewrites
public abstract class AbstractAutoRewrites extends EmptyInputReasoner

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public IReasonerOutput apply(IProverSequent seq, IReasonerInput input, IProofMonitor pm)
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Apply this reasoner to the given sequent with the given input.
      Parameters:
      seq - the sequent to which this reasoner is applied
      input - the input to this reasoner
      pm - a proof monitor (provides cancellation)
      Returns:
      the result of applying this reasoner to the given sequent (either a proof rule or a failure)
      See Also:
      • IProofRule
      • IReasonerFailure

    • postProcessInferredHyp

      protected Collection<Predicate> postProcessInferredHyp(Predicate inferredHyp)
      Process rewritten hypothesis, potentially making further simplifications.

      Returned collection may contain 0, 1 or more predicates.

      It is considered that no post processing occurred, if and only if the returned collection is of size 1 and contains the given rewritten hypothesis instance.

      Parameters:
      inferredHyp - the rewritten hypothesis
      Returns:
      a collection of hypotheses

    • recursiveRewrite

      private Predicate recursiveRewrite(Predicate pred, IFormulaRewriter rewriter)
      An utility method which try to rewrite a predicate recursively until reaching a fix-point.

      If no rewrite where performed on this predicate, then a reference to this predicate is returned (rather than a copy of this predicate). This allows to test efficiently (using ==) whether rewriting made any change.

      Parameters:
      pred - the input predicate
      Returns:
      the resulting predicate after rewrite.

    • getRewriter

      protected abstract IFormulaRewriter getRewriter()

    • getDisplayName

      protected abstract String getDisplayName()
